page: Amazon Web Services m9gd.metal-48xl is a bare-metal, general-purpose server powered by AWS Graviton processors. It features 192 dedicated physical cores on the arm64 architecture, utilizing the ARMv9 Neoverse-V3 model running at 2.8 GHz. The processor configuration includes 64 KB L1d cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 96 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, the instance includes 11,400 GB of NVMe SSD instance store volumes. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 100 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core and memory performance. The stress-ng multi-core CPU test and PassMark CPU score both rank in the top 10%, reflecting the high parallel processing capacity of the 192 dedicated cores. Memory bandwidth is also in the top 10%, with read-only large block speeds reaching 725.79 GB/sec and write speeds at 330.7 GB/sec. However, single-core performance is more moderate, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance falling into the middle 50%. Additionally, while LLM prompt processing speeds for a 7B model rank in the top 10% at 381.5 tokens/sec, text generation speeds for the same model average 15.85 tokens/sec, and drop to the bottom 10% at 27.04 tokens/sec for a smaller 135M model, highlighting a performance tradeoff between input processing and output generation.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the bare-metal architecture and high resource density of the ARMv9 Neoverse-V3 cores offer a favorable performance-per-watt profile for highly parallelized workloads. The combination of 11,400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and top-tier database operation benchmarks makes this server suitable for large-scale databases, high-throughput Redis deployments, static web serving, and multi-threaded decompression tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services m9gd.metal-48xl is a bare-metal, general-purpose server utilizing 192 dedicated ARMv9 Neoverse-V3 cores on an arm64 architecture. Operating at 2.8 GHz, it features 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory and 11,400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, alongside a 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results place its multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations in the top 10% tier. However, single-core CPU performance remains in the middle 50%, and LLM text generation speeds show a notable performance tradeoff compared to prompt processing. This server is structured for highly parallelized workloads, including large-scale database operations, high-throughput Redis deployments, static web serving, and multi-threaded decompression tasks.

tagline: A bare-metal arm64 platform combining 192 dedicated cores with 11.4 TB of local NVMe storage for high-throughput parallel workloads.
